Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a Windows activity system log We need to transform it into a process mining event log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case Identification We need to group events into cases A case represents a logical unit of user work

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the log we see the user working on multiple documents and tasks However note that the user switches between applications and documents

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The log starts with focusing on Quarterly Report docx but then switches to Document1 docx and starts typing Then the user saves Document1 switches to Chrome to handle an email then switches to a PDF then to Excel for budget then back to Document1 then closes Document1 and finally works on Quarterly Report again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Option 1 Each document or task could be a case But note that the user works on Document1 then interrupts it to do email and other tasks This suggests that Document1 is a single case that spans multiple time intervals

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Option 2 We might consider a case as a continuous work session But the log spans about 8 minutes and the user is clearly working on multiple things

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests that the user is multitasking Therefore we should break the log into separate cases for each task

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Proposed cases Case Drafting Document1 docx Case Email Handling Case PDF Review Case Budget Update Case Quarterly Report Editing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The initial focus on Quarterly Report is very brief It might be that the user opened it and then decided to work on Document1 first Then at the end the user comes back to Quarterly Report

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we can have one case for Quarterly Report that includes both the initial focus and the later editing because it is the same document

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to map the raw actions to activity names

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We want to map these to higher level activities For example FOCUS could be Edit Document TYPING could be Edit Content SAVE could be Save Document

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For email CLICK events we can differentiate by the action attribute Open Email Start Reply Send Email

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: SCROLL can be Scroll View HIGHLIGHT can be Highlight Text CLOSE can be Close Document

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The SWITCH event is not directly related to a case It is a navigation event Therefore I propose to ignore SWITCH events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The instructions do not say to include every raw event They say each event in the final log should correspond to a meaningful activity in a process instance

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore if we do not find a meaningful activity for SWITCH we skip it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need at least the attributes Case ID Activity Name Timestamp

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We may include additional attributes such as Application and Window Title

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Line by line transformation assigns Quarterly Report events to the Quarterly Report case Document1 events to the Document1 case email events to the Email case PDF events to the PDF case and Excel events to the Budget case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The case for Document1 Editing includes two separate editing sessions with an interruption because it is the same document

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly the case for Quarterly Report Editing includes an initial focus and a later editing session

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We skipped lines 6 12 19 SWITCH

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We grouped events into cases based on the document or task being performed Each case corresponds to a logical unit of work editing a specific document handling an email reviewing a PDF updating a budget

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The event log is suitable for process mining as it has a clear case structure and standardized activities
